query: PostgreSQL TimescaleDB Citus real-time analytics 50000 events per second architecture
answer: TimescaleDB and Citus enable real-time analytics at scale, handling up to 50,000 events per second through parallel processing and sharding. They optimize data partitioning and use columnar storage for fast query performance. Both solutions ensure high availability and scalability for real-time data analytics.

results.title: Real-Time Analytics & Massively Parallel Postgres - Citus Data
results.snippet: Customer-facing analytics dashboards often require sub-second response times. Because Citus parallelizes SQL over multiple nodes, Citus can provide real-time responsiveness, even with terabytes of data. Moreover, Citus can pre-aggregate the data in parallel across all servers using standard SQL commands to enable millisecond query times even with billions of events per day. Because of the intelligent way Citus distributes SQL over multiple nodes, you get massive parallelism AND concurrency, with Citus coordinating hundreds or even thousands of cores for high performance data ingest and fast analytical queries. #### Get Insanely Fast Analytics Without Re-Architecting Your App. One of our customers describes Citus as insanely fast for real-time analytics (yay.) There are other benefits as well. ## Why Citus for Real-Time Analytics Dashboards? 2. Need to enable customers to analyze new events & new data as it happens, in real-time. ## Learn More About Citus & Real-Time Analytics Apps. Real-time event aggregation at scale using Postgres w/Citus.

results.title: TigerData Architecture for Real-Time Analytics
results.snippet: TigerData achieves this through the following optimiza-tions: • Efficient data partitioning: Automatically and trans-parently partitioning data into chunks, ensuring fast queries, minimal indexing overhead, and seamless scalability • Row-columnar storage: Providing the flexibility of a row store for transactions and the performance of a column store for analytics • Optimized query execution: Using techniques like chunk and batch exclusion, columnar storage, and vectorized execution to minimize latency • Continuous aggregates: Precomputing analytical re-sults for fast insights without expensive reprocessing • Cloud-native operation: Compute/compute separa-tion, elastic usage-based storage, horizontal scale out, data tiering to object storage • Operational simplicity Offering high availability, connection pooling, and automated backups for reli-able and scalable real-time applications With TigerData, developers can build low-latency, high-concurrency applications that seamlessly handle streaming data, historical queries, and real-time analytics while lever-aging the familiarity and power of PostgreSQL. 2.1 Efficient data partitioning TimescaleDB provides hypertables (Figure 1), a table abstrac-tion that automatically partitions data into chunks in real time (using time stamps or incrementing IDs) to ensure fast queries and predictable performance as datasets grow.

results.title: Real-Time Dashboards At Scale: How Citus For PostgreSQL Powers High-Velocity Analytics
results.snippet: # Real-Time Dashboards at Scale: How Citus for PostgreSQL Powers High-Speed Analytics. Real-Time Dashboards at Scale: How Citus for PostgreSQL Powers High-Speed Analytics. Teams building real-time dashboards usually notice the first signs of strain when PostgreSQL starts juggling nonstop writes and frequent analytical queries at once. Citus transforms PostgreSQL by dividing tables into shards and distributing those shards across worker nodes. Citus for PostgreSQL supports these pipelines by letting workers ingest data in parallel. Teams running Citus on ScaleGrid often pair this observability with automated scaling and alerting, ensuring that clusters stay responsive during peak periods and dashboards continue to deliver accurate, real-time insight even under heavy load. Once a cluster grows, engineers must keep an eye on shard placement, monitor coordinator and worker replicas, track replication lag, manage storage distribution, and tune the system so dashboard queries remain steady as volume rises. ScaleGrid provides a managed PostgreSQL platform with out-of-the-box Citus support, tailored to distributed workloads.

results.title: Timescale 2025: Scaling Real-Time Analytics in Postgres - DEV Community
results.snippet: # Timescale 2025: Scaling Real-Time Analytics in Postgres. Timescale unveils innovations for scaling Postgres for real-time analytics. ## Scaling Postgres for Real-Time Analytics on Time-Series Data: The Challenge. Postgres is one of the most powerful and flexible databases available, but as real-time analytics workloads grow, traditional approaches start to fail. To date, we’ve made Postgres the best database for real-time analytics—eliminating the need for specialized infrastructure. Now, in 2025, we’re pushing even further—removing bottlenecks, reducing query latency, and proving that Postgres can scale to petabytes of data and trillions of metrics—all in a single instance. This week, we’re unveiling the next wave of innovations that make Postgres even more powerful for real-time analytics. This was one of the most requested features for TimescaleDB—and on Thursday, we’ll show you how it can supercharge your real-time analytics workflows. Just Postgres for real-time analytics at scale. By the end of the week, you’ll have a complete picture of how Timescale eliminates bottlenecks, scales to petabytes, and brings the speed of transactional databases to analytics workloads.
